text: Polarized training intensity distribution is superior to other training intensity distributions for improving VO2peak, with a small but statistically significant effect size.
Polarized training's superiority for VO2peak improvement is only evident in shorter interventions under 12 weeks, not in longer training periods.
Polarized training's VO2peak benefit is more pronounced in highly trained athletes compared to lower-trained individuals.
Polarized training does not improve time-trial performance more than other training intensity distributions.
Polarized training does not improve speed or power at the lactate/ventilatory threshold more than other training intensity distributions.
Polarized training more effectively improves aerobic power (VO2peak) but is similar to other intensity distributions for improving aerobic capacity (threshold and time-trial performance).
Polarized training does not improve time to exhaustion more than other training intensity distributions, though the trend favors polarized.
